item.title: approval-queue.json written without mode 0600 — pending agent action submissions world-readable
item.intent: approval-queue.json MUST be written with mode 0600 — pending action submissions can contain sensitive content (email text, message content, transaction details) and must not be world-readable.
item.description: Two code paths write approval-queue.json without a file mode, leaving it world-readable (default 0644). CODE PATH 1: src/evolve/approval/queue.ts line 59 calls 'await writeFile(queuePath(deployDir), JSON.stringify(queue, null, 2))' with no mode option. CODE PATH 2: src/design/tools/approve-action.ts (the generated tool template) line 62 calls 'writeFileSync(QUEUE_FILE, JSON.stringify(queue, null, 2))' with no mode option — QUEUE_FILE = join(__dirname, '..', 'memory', 'approval-queue.json'). The approval queue stores high-stakes pending action submissions: email content, message text, account changes, purchase details — whatever the agent submitted for user approval. Any user on the system can read pending action submissions. Fix for CODE PATH 1: change writeFile call to writeFile(queuePath(deployDir), JSON.stringify(queue, null, 2), { mode: 0o600 }). Fix for CODE PATH 2: in the generated code template string, change writeFileSync(QUEUE_FILE, JSON.stringify(queue, null, 2)) to writeFileSync(QUEUE_FILE, JSON.stringify(queue, null, 2), { mode: 0o600 }). No import needed for either fix — 0o600 literal is sufficient.
steps: Read src/evolve/approval/queue.ts lines 53-60 — confirm writeFile call has no mode option

Change 'await writeFile(queuePath(deployDir), JSON.stringify(queue, null, 2))' to 'await writeFile(queuePath(deployDir), JSON.stringify(queue, null, 2), { mode: 0o600 })' — no import needed, 0o600 literal is sufficient
Read src/design/tools/approve-action.ts lines 58-67 — confirm writeFileSync in generated template has no mode option

In the generated code template string, change 'writeFileSync(QUEUE_FILE, JSON.stringify(queue, null, 2))' to 'writeFileSync(QUEUE_FILE, JSON.stringify(queue, null, 2), { mode: 0o600 })' — no import needed, 0o600 literal in generated CJS code
Run npm test -- --reporter=verbose 2>&1 | tail -20 to confirm all tests pass
acceptanceCriteria: src/evolve/approval/queue.ts writeFile call includes { mode: 0o600 } option
src/design/tools/approve-action.ts generated template writeFileSync call includes { mode: 0o600 } option
approval-queue.json is written with mode 0600 — stat confirms no group/other read bits
